entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary crop studied for yield and performance in the experiment.

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A sustainable farming approach aimed at addressing soil degradation and fertility decline.

entities.name: NO-TILL
entities.description: A cropping system practice that avoids soil disturbance, used as a basis for the experiment.

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the multi-location and multi-year experiment was conducted.

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality that conservation agriculture and no-till systems aim to address.

entities.name: FERTILITY DECLINE
entities.description: The reduction in soil nutrients that conservation agriculture and no-till systems aim to address.

entities.name: MAIZE RESIDUE
entities.description: Biomass from maize plants applied to the soil surface as mulch.

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: Mineral nitrogen applied to the soil to enhance maize performance.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of maize productivity used to assess the effects of residue and nitrogen.

entities.name: LEAF CHLOROPHYLL CONTENT
entities.description: A physiological measure of maize performance used in some sites.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The seasonal precipitation pattern that influenced the effect of residue levels on grain yield.

entities.name: AGRO-ECOLOGICAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: The diverse environmental settings across the sites in southern Africa.

relationships.description: has limited or variable effect on
influences the effect of residue on yield
